# @tilia/query

> Offline-first query and cache layer for Tilia apps: normalized object cache,
> two-tier reads (local store + remote), durable optimistic write/delete
> outbox with reconnect replay, reactive sync status, stale refresh, and
> reactivity-driven garbage collection.

Key facts for code generation:

- Create an instance with `make(config)` where config is a single options
  object: `{id, remote, local?, stale?, gc?, now?, key?, matches?, sort?}`.
- `matches(query, value)` is the membership predicate: writes and `sync`
  update query id-lists in place (no refetch). `sort(a, b)` keeps id-lists
  sorted and stable, so views only change when membership changes.
- Reads: `one(query)`, `array(query)`, `dict(query)` return a loadable:
  the string "loading", the string "notFound", or `{state: "loaded", data}`.
  Array queries never resolve to "notFound"; empty results are loaded.
- Writes: `upsert(item)` and `remove(item)` are optimistic and durable; they
  are pushed to the remote when online and replayed on reconnect and after
  restart.
- Adapters respond through named channel callbacks, never tagged values:
  fetch channels have `emit(rows)`, `fail(message)`, `covered()`; write
  channels have `emit(saved)`, `offline()`, `conflict(server)`,
  `reject(message)`.
- The `remote` adapter must be a tilia object with a reactive `online`
  boolean; the core watches it to replay the outbox on reconnect. `make`
  throws if `remote` is not a tilia proxy.
- The optional `local` store answers every query offline and persists the
  outbox: `save(item, dirty)`, `remove(item, dirty)` (dirty=true writes a
  delete tombstone), `dirty()` returns `{value, deleted}` records.
- `status` is a reactive object: `pending` (outbox size), `rejected`
  (refused writes, cleared with `dismiss()`), `error` (last fetch failure).
- The library never starts timers: call `tick()` from your own scheduler for
  stale refresh and garbage collection. Call `clear()` on logout (memory
  only; wipe the local database yourself) and `dispose()` to shut down.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): full API tour with REST remote and Dexie local store recipes (TypeScript)
- [Technical overview](docs/technical.md): contracts, data flow, settlement matrix, delta-sync compatibility
- [Vision](docs/vision.md): why TiliaQuery exists
- [ReScript contract](src/TiliaQuery.resi): source of truth for the API
- [TypeScript types](src/index.d.ts): TS mirror of the contract
- [Tilia docs](https://tiliajs.com): the reactive core this builds on
